However in my view the Naga avenue of Venus is constituted
by the three asterisms Svatx, Bharanl and Krttika; and the Gaja,
Airavata and Vrsabkahy the three succeeding triads of stars, viz. (1)
RohinI, Mrgasiras, and Ardra, (2) Punarvasu, Pusya and Asle§a,

98 Brhai Samhita
(3) Magha, Purvaphalgun! and Uttaraphalguni. And the Govlthl
is composed of the four asterisms Asvini, Revati, Purvabhadra and
Uttarabhadra.
«r^!TT^r^ ^ \
Similarly the jaradgava avenue is composed of three stars
Sravana, Dhani§tha and Satabhisaj; the Mrga avenue of Anuradha,
Jyestha and Mula, the Ajavithl of Hasta, Citra and Visakha; and
the Dahana avenue of the two stars Parvasa^ha and Uttarasadha.
[Our author and the ancients are of the same view only in
respect of the three Vithis, viz. Gaja, Airavata and Vrsabha.]
n V II
Of these nine avenues the first three viz. NUga, Gaja and
Airavata, are in the North', the next three viz. Vrsabha, Go and jarad-
gava, in the Middle; and the last three viz.
